channel.title: Morning Briefing for George
channel.description: Personalized daily briefing covering business, markets, and industry news. Single-listener feed.
channel.itunes:author: George Cherian
channel.itunes:summary: Personalized daily briefing covering business, markets, and industry news. Single-listener feed.

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, May 12
item.description: In world business, oil is the dominant story. Prices extended gains this morning after President Trump described the ceasefire with Iran as on "life support," raising concerns about a prolonged…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, May 11
item.description: In world business, two stories are driving the macro picture this morning. First, oil. Brent crude has crossed one hundred and three dollars a barrel after the Trump administration rejected Iran's…

item.title: Briefing — Friday, May 8
item.description: In world business, three stories are shaping the macro picture. Oil prices moved higher after a military exchange between US and Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz — President Trump says the…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, May 7
item.description: In world business, the conflict with Iran is reshaping energy markets in real time. Shell posted a nearly twenty-five percent jump in quarterly profits, beating estimates as the Iran war drove…

item.title: Briefing — Wednesday, May 6
item.description: In world business, the big macro signal overnight is oil. Brent crude dropped to around one hundred one dollars a barrel after President Trump announced a pause in U.S. naval escort operations in…

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, May 5
item.description: In world business, Australia's central bank has raised rates again and is warning that inflation will stay elevated longer than expected, citing a pickup in price pressures through the second half…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, May 4
item.description: In world business, oil prices are moving higher after a tanker was struck by projectiles north of Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates. The UK Maritime Trade Operations agency confirmed the incident…

item.title: Briefing — Friday, May 1
item.description: In world business, oil prices dipped after reports that Iran sent a response to a draft peace agreement — a signal that diplomatic channels are still open, which tends to ease supply-risk premiums…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, April 30
item.description: In world business, Apple posted seventeen percent revenue growth in its second quarter, beating estimates even as iPhone unit sales came in below expectations. The real driver was services — a…
